A former Japanese school principal, Yuhei Takashima, admitted to police that he paid for sex with over 12,000 women, some as young as 14, during repeated visits to the Philippines spanning 25 years. Police in Japan arrested Takashima for producing child pornography and seized 147,600 photos of his activities. The case had been under investigation since 2013, with Takeshi Akimoto, a police officer, confirming the seizure. Takashima worked at a Japanese school in Manila between 1988 and 1991, later serving as a junior high school principal until his retirement in 2011. Yokohama education authorities expressed regret over the allegations if proven true.

A Brazilian online store is selling alleged next season's Chelsea kits, including one with Gareth Bale's name and number 9, ahead of the new 'Yokohama' sponsor. The store has also published three such kits without the Adidas emblem, amid reports linking Bale to a Premier League return from Spain. Meanwhile, Drogba shirts are also available for sale despite his uncertain future at Chelsea.
